pattern: After sorting, every split point divides the array into a prefix raised and a suffix lowered, and the best partition minimizes the resulting spread among a linear number of candidate cuts.
hints: Each value must move by the full amount in one direction or the other, so the only freedom is which elements go up and which go down.
Once the array is sorted, an optimal choice always raises a prefix and lowers the remaining suffix; only the boundary between them varies.

Sort, then for each cut between index i and i+1 the candidate spread is max(last_raised, last) - min(first, first_lowered); scan all cuts and keep the smallest, starting from the trivial no-cut spread.

edge_inputs.description: two elements where raising both keeps the original gap
the clever split beats the naive max-down min-up answer
zero shift leaves the spread untouched
already sorted with equal gaps stresses the boundary scan
large k relative to small values

stress.description: random arrays with random shift so the optimal cut is rarely at the extremes
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, 5000)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: forces the prefix/suffix split logic across many boundaries
